mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals a labor market inflection that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and residential housing starts indicates synchronized private sector expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline GDPC1-only model in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or forward returns turn negative when HOUST growth decelerates despite rising GDPC1.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the industrial production index above its 6-month moving average reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which reduces input cost volatility and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO crosses its moving average, or the signal generates negative returns during periods of rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail and food services sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon without requiring additional macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 150,000 jobs per month and stable federal funds rates signal a healthy labor market operating within accommodative monetary policy, which maximizes equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are not significantly higher when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k but FEDFUNDS rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls indicates a supply-side productivity shock that expands profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week during early-cycle recoveries.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative when INDPRO and UNRATE move in the specified directions, or the signal fails to outperform a single-proxy INDPRO model in out-of-sample testing.
